reviews.text:
I feel it’s important to share this experience. From one hardworking business owner to another, I strongly recommend slowing down, reading this review carefully, and rethinking working with this company.

From the start, my experience with Cohen Healthcare Law Group felt off. The initial sales calls were extremely pushy, which in hindsight should have been a red flag.

Once I began working with the attorney assigned to me, it became clear that he did not take the time to truly understand what he was advising me on—specifically regarding consent forms that are critical to my business. I often found myself questioning guidance that was later walked back, leaving me feeling like I was guiding my own counsel rather than being advised.

At this point, I am $3,500 in and do not feel confident that I have a usable or properly drafted consent form. Incorrect information was included in the document, and despite three follow-up attempts over nearly three weeks, I have not received a response. I have also since been contacted regarding a potential overage charge, which is especially frustrating given the lack of completion.

I’ve owned and operated my business for nearly 10 years and have worked with many professionals. This has been one of the most disappointing experiences I’ve had, and based on my experience, I would not recommend this firm.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Dear Donna,

Thank you for taking the time to share your experience. I want you to know that we take feedback like yours seriously, and I appreciate the honesty in what you wrote. As a fellow business owner, I understand the weight and frustration that comes with feeling like expectations were not met, especially when those expectations are tied to critical aspects of your operations and compliance.

I’d like to address a few parts of your experience directly.

First, regarding the intake and sales process: you are right to expect clarity and alignment in those early stages. Legal services are not transactional, and no one should walk into an engagement feeling pushed or pressured. That is never the intention of our intake team. Their role is to understand the prospective client’s goals, determine whether we are the right fit, and ensure that expectations are aligned before any work begins. If you walked away feeling rushed or sold to, then we missed the mark, and for that I apologize.

Second, regarding the substantive legal work and communication: you mentioned feeling as though you were guiding your own attorney and receiving walked-back guidance related to consent forms. Consent forms and healthcare compliance documents sit at the intersection of regulatory law, risk management, professional ethics, and clinical workflow. They must be drafted with precision, and they must be understood in the context of the specific business model, state regulations, and treatment protocols. If you felt that the required depth was not being applied, that is an issue worth examining closely. Confidence in legal counsel matters, particularly for operators in the healthcare space. Unresponsiveness and delays only compound that concern, and three weeks without a reply to follow-up questions is not acceptable. That is not the standard we set internally, nor the experience we want our clients to have.

Third, regarding fees and the overage conversation: legal work in the healthcare compliance category can involve different levels of research, drafting, and revision depending on scope. That said, the conversation around overages should never outpace the work itself or exist in tension with responsiveness or completion. Hearing about potential additional fees when the original deliverable is not yet where it needs to be understandably feels misaligned.

Most importantly, I want to acknowledge how you felt reading back your own words: unconfident in your documents, unheard in your follow-ups, and disappointed in the overall experience. When a client walks away feeling that way, even one client, it signals something to learn from. It means we need to examine communication standards, internal workflows, feedback loops between our legal and operations teams, and whether our structure supported your matter in the way it should have.

While I cannot undo that experience retroactively, I do want to extend an invitation to resolve this properly. If you would be willing, I would like to review your matter personally from start to finish, examine the substantive legal work completed to date, understand the context of your consent form concerns, and look at where communication broke down. If additional work is needed to complete the deliverable to the standard you expected, we can discuss that path as well, but either way, resolution should be grounded in fairness and integrity.

Every law firm has a mandate to do right by the people who entrust them with their legal matters. Your review reminds us of that mandate. We are not above criticism, and we do not shy away from opportunities to improve client experience, operational clarity, and communication.

If you are open to it, please reach out directly so we can close this loop in a way that honors the time, money, and trust you invested.

Thank you again for speaking candidly. I hear you.

Respectfully,
Phil Blende, M.A., M.B.A.
Chief Operating Officer
Cohen Healthcare Law Group, PC

I am told she is no longer there, but she was very Kind and answered all of my questions, however she gave me some bad advice.  She was mistaken and told me that in the state of Ohio you do not need a TDDD license to own an IV Hydration clinic and that is not true.... You DO NEED this license and the Dr that I hired didn't have one and wouldn't get one so I had already built this business and now couldn't get the products that I needed.  I will say that everyone at Cohen Healthcare law group did whatever they could do to make it right.  Clara the COO of the company called me personally and was sincere, and kind, Chis is another lawyer that helped to make it right.  Although Molly had made a mistake,  you could tell that the people there really cared about me and my business,  you could tell they were not just covering their butts... but, truly cared. reviews.text:
I facilitated my brother in law to speak to Robert.  He needed the prod. For a small legal fee,my brother in law benefitted $35,000.

I talked to a number of attorneys and they said we did not have a case.  When we spoke Robert he agreed we did not have strong legal case but suggested my brother in law call and then write a follow-up email to HR to explain his legitimate family situation.  He said sometimes this really works and a phone call is more personal and is better do in first before an email  The HR Director empathized with my brother in laws situation and to make a long story short, it benefitted him about $35,000.  Previously a friend told my brother in law to file a harassment charge instead.   Robert said that had no basis and would not work with an empathy strategy.  So that was invaluable to know. He spent time outlining the key elements for the phone call and letter.  Many things we did not think about.  Probably because he negotiates for a living.  Now it is true he did not do any legal action but it is also true that he applied his experience to find an alternative plan that was definitely worth trying.  We knew this was no guarantee but it worked great.  And if we went the harrassment route, it would have totally backfired.  So we highly recommend Robert as he thinks outside the box.  The other attorneys made assumptions and were not solution minded.  And it did not cost me much in legal fees.  Totally worth it.
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you, Jason, for your kind words. I do my best to find a solution that works for each client. One size definitely does not fit all, and I am glad to hear things worked out well.

reviews.text:
Mr. Nuddleman man is very helpful and precise, he helped us file an UI appeal and had coached us how to argue our case by law, we ended winning our case with his help.
"Mehdi"
reviews.ownerResponse: I’m glad I was able to help.

reviews.text: I was extremely fortunate to have another attorney in the field of employment law recommend Mr. Nuddleman's services. His ability to examine a complex situation and bring clarity to the playing field is second to none. He is efficient, patient, thorough and dedicated to the best possible outcome for his clients. Mr. Nuddleman's knowledge of his field is highly evident and his ability to educate his clients about the legal nuances relevant to their situation is exceptional. Without reservation I would recommend Mr. Nuddleman to any employer or employee seeking guidance or counsel.
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you so much for your comment. It is always uplifting to hear that I was able to assist others.
